description_long: This rifle features a classic design with a wooden fixed stock and foregrip, paired with a black metal receiver and barrel. The magazine is a curved, detachable box type, typical of mid-20th-century military rifles. The rifle is equipped with iron sights, including a front post and a rear notch, and the muzzle is fitted with a simple flash hider. The wood components have a polished, reddish-brown finish, contrasting with the matte black metal parts. The overall build suggests durability and functionality, with ergonomic shaping for comfortable handling. The rifle's proportions and details indicate it is modeled after a historical assault rifle, likely the Sturmgewehr 44 (StG 44).
description: A classic rifle with a wooden fixed stock, black metal receiver, curved magazine, and iron sights, featuring a flash hider at the muzzle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the barrel end, front sight post, and flash hider, with the wooden stock and magazine barely visible.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full length of the rifle, including the wooden stock, curved magazine, trigger, and iron sights.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel, with the magazine and sights seen end-on.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the rifle's profile, including the wooden stock, curved magazine, trigger, and iron sights.
